name: Hawa Mahal
description: The Palace of Winds is a stunning pink sandstone structure with 953 small windows designed to allow royal women to observe street life. Built in 1799 by Maharaja Sawai Pratap Singh.

name: Amber Fort
description: A majestic fort palace built from red sandstone and marble, known for its artistic Hindu style elements. The fort overlooks Maota Lake and offers incredible views.

name: City Palace Jaipur
description: A magnificent blend of Rajasthani and Mughal architecture in the heart of the old city. Houses a museum with royal costumes, armory, and art.

name: Jantar Mantar Jaipur
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring the world's largest stone sundial. Built by Maharaja Jai Singh II to observe astronomical positions.

name: Nahargarh Fort
description: Perched on the Aravalli Hills, this fort offers panoramic views of Jaipur city. The sunset view from here is spectacular.

name: Jal Mahal
description: A beautiful palace sitting in the middle of Man Sagar Lake. While you cannot enter, the views from the shore are mesmerizing, especially at sunset.

name: Albert Hall Museum
description: The oldest museum of Rajasthan, housing a rich collection of paintings, carpets, ivory, metal sculptures, and crystal works.

name: Birla Mandir Jaipur
description: A stunning white marble Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu. The temple is beautifully illuminated at night.

name: Jaigarh Fort
description: Known as the 'Fort of Victory', houses the world's largest cannon on wheels - Jaivana. Connected to Amber Fort via underground passages.

name: Chokhi Dhani
description: An ethnic village resort offering authentic Rajasthani thali, folk dances, puppet shows, and camel rides. A complete cultural dining experience.

name: LMB (Laxmi Mishthan Bhandar)
description: Iconic vegetarian restaurant since 1727 famous for its Rajasthani thali, dal baati churma, and ghevar sweets.

name: Bapu Bazaar
description: A bustling market famous for textiles, bangles, mojari shoes, and handicrafts. Perfect for souvenir shopping.

name: Johri Bazaar
description: One of the oldest and most famous jewelry markets in India. Known for kundan, meenakari jewelry, and precious gemstones.

name: Central Park Jaipur
description: A vast green space in the heart of the city with jogging tracks, a musical fountain, and India's tallest national flag.

name: Gateway of India
description: An iconic arch monument built in the Indo-Saracenic style overlooking the Arabian Sea. Built to commemorate the visit of King George V in 1911.

name: Marine Drive
description: A 3.6 km long boulevard along the coastline known as the Queen's Necklace for its nighttime lighting. Perfect for evening walks.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. A historic Victorian Gothic railway station with stunning stained glass windows and ornate stone carvings.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with rock-cut cave temples dating back to 5th-8th centuries. Features magnificent sculptures of Lord Shiva.

name: Siddhivinayak Temple
description: One of the most visited Hindu temples in Mumbai dedicated to Lord Ganesha. The temple dome is gold-plated and is considered very auspicious.

name: Juhu Beach
description: Mumbai's most famous beach known for street food stalls, Bollywood celebrity spotting, and beautiful sunsets over the Arabian Sea.

name: Haji Ali Dargah
description: An iconic mosque and tomb built on an islet 500 meters into the Arabian Sea, accessible only during low tide via a narrow causeway.

name: Colaba Causeway
description: A vibrant street market selling everything from antiques to fashion accessories. Also has trendy cafes and bookstores.

name: Leopold Cafe
description: An iconic cafe since 1871 in Colaba, famous for its history and ambiance. Featured in the novel Shantaram.

name: Bademiya
description: Legendary street-side kebab stall near Colaba known for its seekh kebabs, chicken tikka rolls, and late-night dining culture.

name: Sanjay Gandhi National Park
description: A sprawling national park within the city featuring the Kanheri Caves, hiking trails, a lion safari, and rich biodiversity.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya
description: Premier museum of Mumbai housing artifacts from the Indus Valley Civilization, Indian miniature paintings, and decorative art.

name: Taj Mahal
description: One of the Seven Wonders of the World. An ivory-white marble mausoleum built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

name: Agra Fort
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. A massive red sandstone fort that served as the main residence of Mughal emperors until 1638.

name: Fatehpur Sikri
description: A deserted Mughal city built by Emperor Akbar. Home to the beautiful Buland Darwaza, the highest gateway in the world.

name: Mehtab Bagh
description: A Mughal garden across the Yamuna offering the most spectacular sunset views of the Taj Mahal.

name: Itmad-ud-Daulah
description: Often called the Baby Taj, this marble tomb is considered a draft of the Taj Mahal. Features beautiful pietra dura inlay work.

name: Pinch of Spice Agra
description: Popular multi-cuisine restaurant known for its North Indian food, especially the butter chicken, dal makhani, and kebabs.

name: India Gate
description: A war memorial dedicated to Indian soldiers who died in World War I. The surrounding lawns are popular for evening picnics.

name: Red Fort
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. A massive red sandstone fort built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan. The PM hoists the flag here on Independence Day.

name: Qutub Minar
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. The tallest brick minaret in the world at 72.5 meters, built in 1193 by Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

name: Humayun's Tomb
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. The first garden tomb in the Indian subcontinent, which inspired the Taj Mahal.

name: Lotus Temple
description: A Bahá'í House of Worship shaped like a lotus flower, made of white marble. Open to all faiths for meditation and prayer.

name: Akshardham Temple
description: A massive Hindu temple complex showcasing 10,000 years of Indian culture through exhibitions, boat rides, and musical fountains.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: One of the oldest and busiest markets in Old Delhi. Famous for spices, dry fruits, fabrics, jewelry, and legendary street food.

name: Karim's
description: A legendary Mughlai restaurant near Jama Masjid since 1913. Known for nihari, mutton burra, and biryani.

name: Paranthe Wali Gali
description: A narrow lane in Chandni Chowk famous for its deep-fried stuffed paranthas served since the 1870s with various chutneys.

name: Lodhi Garden
description: A beautiful park spread over 90 acres containing tombs of the Sayyid and Lodhi dynasties. Popular for morning walks and jogging.

name: National Museum Delhi
description: India's largest museum with over 200,000 works of art spanning 5,000 years. Includes Harappan artifacts and Buddhist sculptures.

name: Calangute Beach
description: The Queen of Beaches in Goa. A long stretch of golden sand popular for water sports, sunbathing, and beach shacks.

name: Baga Beach
description: A popular beach known for its energetic nightlife, water sports like parasailing and jet skiing, and famous beach clubs.

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site housing the mortal remains of St. Francis Xavier. A masterpiece of Baroque architecture from 1605.

name: Fort Aguada
description: A well-preserved 17th century Portuguese fort overlooking the Arabian Sea with a four-storey lighthouse.

name: Dudhsagar Falls
description: One of India's tallest waterfalls at 310 meters, set amidst lush forest in the Western Ghats. Accessible via a scenic trek or jeep ride.

name: Anjuna Flea Market
description: A vibrant Wednesday flea market selling jewelry, clothes, spices, and souvenirs. A quintessential Goa experience.

name: Thalassa
description: A Greek restaurant perched on a cliff offering stunning sunset views over the Arabian Sea. Known for its Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Gunpowder Goa
description: A charming restaurant serving South Indian and Kerala cuisine in a countryside setting. Known for its appam, stew, and fish curry.

name: Palolem Beach
description: A crescent-shaped beach in South Goa known for its calm waters, kayaking, and silent discos. More relaxed than North Goa beaches.

name: Ghats of Varanasi
description: Over 80 ghats along the Ganges river. The Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat at sunset is a mesmerizing spiritual experience.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of the most famous Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Shiva. The temple's spire is gold-plated with 800 kg of gold.

name: Sarnath
description: The place where Buddha gave his first sermon. Features the Dhamek Stupa, Ashoka Pillar, and an archaeological museum.

name: Boat Ride on Ganges
description: A sunrise boat ride on the holy Ganges offering views of the ghats, temples, and the morning rituals of the city.

name: Ramnagar Fort
description: An 18th century sandstone fort on the eastern bank of the Ganges. Houses a museum with vintage cars, weapons, and ivory work.

name: Blue Lassi Shop
description: A legendary lassi shop serving over 70 flavors of thick, creamy lassi in terracotta cups since 1925.

name: Kachori Gali
description: A famous lane for hot kachori, jalebi, and chaat. The freshly fried kachori with sabzi is a must-try breakfast.

name: Mysore Palace
description: The seat of the Wodeyar dynasty. A grand Indo-Saracenic palace illuminated by 97,000 lights on Sundays and public holidays.

name: Chamundi Hills
description: A hilltop temple of Goddess Chamundeshwari with 1,000 steps leading to the top. Features a massive Nandi bull statue.

name: Brindavan Gardens
description: Terraced gardens built across the KRS Dam with musical fountains, illuminated in colorful lights in the evening.

name: Devaraja Market
description: A 100+ year old market bursting with colors. Famous for Mysore sandalwood, silk, spices, and fresh flowers.

name: Mylari Dosa Hotel
description: Iconic eatery famous for its signature butter-soaked spongy dosa. A must-visit for any food enthusiast visiting Mysore.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
description: A network of canals, lagoons, and lakes best explored on a traditional houseboat. The Venice of the East.

name: Fort Kochi
description: A historic seaside neighborhood with Portuguese, Dutch, and British colonial architecture, Chinese fishing nets, and art galleries.

name: Chinese Fishing Nets
description: Iconic cantilevered fishing nets introduced by Chinese traders in the 14th century. Best photographed at sunset.

name: Mattancherry Palace
description: Also called the Dutch Palace. Known for stunning Kerala murals depicting scenes from the Ramayana and Mahabharata.

name: Jew Town & Synagogue
description: Home to the Paradesi Synagogue (1568), the oldest active synagogue in the Commonwealth. The surrounding streets are lined with antique shops.

name: Kayees Rahmathulla Hotel
description: Legendary eatery famous for its Malabar biryani. The Kochi biryani here is a must-try with its distinctive spice blend.

name: Munnar Tea Gardens
description: Rolling hills covered with tea plantations offering breathtaking views. Visit the KDHP Tea Museum to learn about tea processing.

name: Eravikulam National Park
description: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr. The park offers stunning montane grasslands and shola forests at 2000m elevation.

name: Mattupetty Dam
description: A scenic dam surrounded by tea gardens and forests. Offers speed boat rides on the reservoir with mountain views.

name: Udaipur City Palace
description: A magnificent palace complex on the banks of Lake Pichola. Features Rajput architecture with courtyards, towers, and gardens.

name: Lake Pichola
description: A beautiful artificial lake surrounded by palaces, temples, and hills. The sunset boat ride is unforgettable.

name: Jag Mandir
description: A palace built on an island in Lake Pichola. Features beautiful gardens and a restaurant with lakeside dining.

name: Saheliyon ki Bari
description: Garden of the Maidens - a beautiful ornamental garden with lotus pools, marble elephants, and fountains.

name: Ambrai Ghat Restaurant
description: A lakeside restaurant offering stunning views of City Palace and Lake Pichola. Known for its Rajasthani thali and tandoori dishes.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
description: One of India's largest and most impressive forts, perched 400 feet above the Blue City. Houses an excellent museum.

name: Umaid Bhawan Palace
description: An Art Deco palace that is still the residence of the Jodhpur royal family. Part of it serves as a Taj hotel and museum.

name: Clock Tower Market
description: A vibrant market around the old clock tower selling spices, textiles, silver jewelry, and handicrafts.

name: Shahi Samosa
description: Famous street food stall near Clock Tower known for its giant crispy samosas and mirchi vada, a Jodhpur specialty.

name: Toorji Ka Jhalra Stepwell
description: A beautifully restored 18th century stepwell with intricate carvings. A peaceful spot surrounded by cafes.

name: Nainital Lake
description: A beautiful crescent-shaped lake surrounded by hills. Enjoy boating on the lake or walk along the scenic Mall Road.

name: Naina Devi Temple
description: Ancient temple on the northern shore of Nainital Lake dedicated to Goddess Naina Devi. Important Shakti Peeth.

name: Snow View Point
description: Accessible by cable car, offers stunning views of snow-capped Himalayan peaks including Nanda Devi and Trishul.

name: Mall Road Nainital
description: The main shopping and walking street along the lake. Lined with restaurants, shops, and hotels.

name: Eco Cave Gardens
description: A series of interconnected caves named after animals - Tiger, Panther, Bat, etc. Fun for adventure seekers.

name: Shimla Ridge
description: A large open space in the heart of Shimla offering views of mountains, hosting cultural events and festivals.

name: Mall Road Shimla
description: The main promenade of Shimla, a car-free zone lined with shops, cafes, and colonial-era buildings.

name: Jakhoo Temple
description: Hilltop temple of Lord Hanuman with a 108-foot statue. The highest point in Shimla with panoramic views.

name: Christ Church Shimla
description: Second oldest church in North India built in 1857. Features beautiful stained glass windows and Neo-Gothic architecture.

name: Kufri
description: A small hill station near Shimla known for skiing in winter, horse riding, and scenic Himalayan views.

name: Indian Coffee House Shimla
description: Iconic heritage cafe on Mall Road known for its colonial ambiance, filter coffee, and mutton cutlets.

name: Hadimba Temple
description: An ancient temple surrounded by deodar forests dedicated to Hadimba Devi. Features unique pagoda-style wooden architecture.

name: Solang Valley
description: An adventure hub known for paragliding, zorbing, skiing in winter, and a ropeway giving panoramic mountain views.

name: Rohtang Pass
description: A high mountain pass at 3,978m offering stunning views of glaciers, peaks, and the Lahaul Valley.

name: Old Manali
description: A charming village with hippie cafes, bakeries, craft shops, and the scenic Manalsu River walks.

name: Vashisht Hot Springs
description: Natural hot water springs near an ancient temple. The village also has Turkish baths for relaxation.

name: Lazy Dog Lounge Manali
description: A cozy cafe in Old Manali known for its wood-fired pizzas, Israeli food, and relaxing mountain-view ambiance.

name: Hampi Ruins
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire spread over 4,100 hectares with stunning boulder-strewn landscape.

name: Virupaksha Temple
description: An active Hindu temple dating back to the 7th century. The main gopuram is 50m tall and features intricate Dravidian carvings.

name: Vittala Temple
description: Famous for the iconic stone chariot and musical pillars that produce musical notes when tapped. Pinnacle of Vijayanagara architecture.

name: Matanga Hill
description: The highest point in Hampi offering 360-degree views of the ruins, river, and boulder landscape. Spectacular sunrise spot.

name: Mango Tree Restaurant Hampi
description: A riverside restaurant under a large mango tree serving thalis, banana pancakes, and fresh juices.

name: Golden Temple
description: The holiest shrine of Sikhism. The gold-plated temple sitting in the Amrit Sarovar is awe-inspiring. The langar feeds 100,000 daily.

name: Jallianwala Bagh
description: Historic garden and memorial commemorating the 1919 massacre. Bullet holes are still visible in the walls.

name: Wagah Border
description: The India-Pakistan border where the evening flag-lowering ceremony draws thousands of spectators with patriotic fervor.

name: Kesar Da Dhaba
description: A century-old dhaba famous for its dal makhani slow-cooked overnight and butter-loaded naan.

name: Kullu Manali
description: A scenic valley along the Beas River with apple orchards, hot springs, and adventure activities like rafting.

name: Rishikesh Lakshman Jhula
description: An iconic suspension bridge over the Ganges at 450 feet. Offers views of ashrams, temples, and the river below.

name: River Rafting Rishikesh
description: White water rafting on the Ganges through Grade 3 and 4 rapids. A top adventure sport destination in India.

name: Triveni Ghat
description: A sacred bathing ghat where three rivers meet. The evening Ganga Aarti here is a peaceful spiritual experience.

name: Beatles Ashram
description: The former Maharishi Mahesh Yogi ashram where The Beatles stayed in 1968. Now features colorful graffiti art.

name: Chotiwala Restaurant
description: An iconic vegetarian restaurant near Ram Jhula known for its thalis, aloo tikki, and the costumed mascot at the entrance.

name: Bangalore Palace
description: A Tudor-style palace inspired by Windsor Castle with beautiful woodcarvings, gardens, and a mix of Gothic and Victorian architecture.

name: Lalbagh Botanical Garden
description: A 240-acre garden with a Glass House, over 1,000 species of plants, a beautiful lake, and India's largest collection of tropical plants.

name: Cubbon Park
description: A sprawling green lung of Bangalore spread over 300 acres with walking paths, a toy train, and colonial-era buildings.

name: ISKCON Temple Bangalore
description: One of the largest ISKCON temples in the world, blending modern architecture with traditional temple design.

name: Vidyarthi Bhavan
description: Legendary since 1943, serving the crispiest butter-soaked masala dosa in Bangalore. Always has a queue.

name: MTR (Mavalli Tiffin Rooms)
description: A heritage restaurant since 1924 famous for its rava idli (which it invented), filter coffee, and full South Indian meals.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: A historic temple with 14 colorful gopurams (gateway towers) covered in thousands of sculpted Hindu deities. A masterpiece of Dravidian architecture.

name: Thirumalai Nayakkar Mahal
description: A 17th century palace with Indo-Saracenic architecture, featuring massive pillars and a grand courtyard.

name: Gandhi Memorial Museum
description: One of five Gandhi museums in India. Houses the blood-stained cloth Mahatma Gandhi wore when he was assassinated.

name: Murugan Idli Shop
description: Famous chain that started in Madurai known for its soft idlis, crispy dosas, and spicy chutneys at budget prices.

name: Pushkar Lake
description: A sacred lake surrounded by 52 ghats. One of the five sacred pilgrimage spots for Hindus. The evening aarti is beautiful.

name: Brahma Temple Pushkar
description: One of the very few temples in the world dedicated to Lord Brahma. A 14th century red spire temple.

name: Savitri Temple
description: A hilltop temple reached by ropeway offering panoramic views of Pushkar Lake and the surrounding desert landscape.

name: Darjeeling Himalayan Railway
description: UNESCO World Heritage toy train running through the mountains. A 2-hour joyride from Darjeeling through loops and zig-zags.

name: Tiger Hill
description: The highest point in Darjeeling at 2590m offering spectacular sunrise views of Kanchenjunga and Mount Everest.

name: Happy Valley Tea Estate
description: One of the oldest tea estates in Darjeeling. Walk through the tea gardens and learn the entire tea-making process.

name: Batasia Loop
description: A spiral railway loop where the toy train navigates a 360-degree turn. Features a war memorial and Kanchenjunga views.

name: Glenary's
description: A heritage bakery and restaurant since 1935. Known for its pastries, continental food, and mountain-view terrace.

name: Ooty Lake
description: An artificial lake surrounded by eucalyptus trees. Enjoy pedal boating with views of the Nilgiri Mountains.

name: Botanical Garden Ooty
description: A stunning 55-acre garden with over 650 species of plants, a fossilized tree trunk, and terraced lawns.

name: Nilgiri Mountain Railway
description: UNESCO World Heritage rack railway. A scenic journey from Mettupalayam through 16 tunnels and 250 bridges.

name: Doddabetta Peak
description: The highest peak in the Nilgiri Mountains at 2637m. Features a telescope house for viewing the surrounding landscape.

name: Hyderabad Charminar
description: The iconic symbol of Hyderabad built in 1591. A mosque with four grand arches and minarets in the center of the old city.

name: Golconda Fort
description: A fortress famous for its acoustic system - a handclap at the gate can be heard at the top. Former diamond trading center.

name: Ramoji Film City
description: The world's largest integrated film city. Features live shows, film sets, adventure activities, and themed gardens.

name: Chowmahalla Palace
description: The seat of the Asaf Jahi dynasty. Elaborately decorated with Belgian crystal chandeliers and vintage cars.

name: Salar Jung Museum
description: One of the largest one-man collections in the world. Features the famous Veiled Rebecca sculpture and a musical clock.

name: Paradise Biryani
description: The most famous biryani restaurant in Hyderabad since 1953. The Hyderabadi dum biryani is a must-try.

name: Shah Ghouse
description: Famous for its late-night Haleem, kebabs, and paya. A Hyderabad institution for non-vegetarian food lovers.

name: Hussain Sagar Lake
description: A heart-shaped lake with a 16m monolithic Buddha statue on Gibraltar Rock in the center. Enjoy boat rides to the statue.

name: Howrah Bridge
description: An iconic cantilever bridge over the Hooghly River. One of the busiest bridges in the world, carrying 100,000 vehicles daily.

name: Victoria Memorial
description: A grand white marble building with a museum dedicated to Queen Victoria. The gardens and light-and-sound show are beautiful.

name: Indian Museum Kolkata
description: The oldest and largest museum in India founded in 1814. Houses Egyptian mummies, Mughal paintings, and dinosaur fossils.

name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
description: A famous temple on the banks of the Hooghly River associated with Ramakrishna Paramahansa. Features 12 Shiva temples.

name: Park Street
description: The food and nightlife hub of Kolkata lined with restaurants, pubs, and bookstores. Christmas celebrations here are legendary.

name: Peter Cat
description: An iconic Park Street restaurant since 1958 famous for its Chelo Kebab - a sizzling platter of kebabs with rice and egg.

name: Flurys
description: An iconic tearoom since 1927 on Park Street. Famous for its pastries, rum balls, and colonial charm.

name: New Market Kolkata
description: A sprawling Victorian-era market selling everything from clothes to electronics to fresh produce. Great for bargain hunters.

name: Mahabalipuram Shore Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site. A 7th century structural temple overlooking the Bay of Bengal with stunning Dravidian architecture.

name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple
description: A stunning Dravidian temple dedicated to Lord Shiva in Mylapore. Features a 37m gopuram covered in colorful sculptures.

name: Marina Beach
description: The second longest urban beach in the world. Famous for evening walks, horse rides, and street food stalls.

name: Government Museum Chennai
description: India's second oldest museum with an excellent collection of South Indian bronzes, Roman antiquities, and Buddhist sculptures.

name: San Thome Basilica
description: A Neo-Gothic church built over the tomb of Saint Thomas the Apostle. One of only three churches in the world built over an apostle's tomb.

name: Saravana Bhavan
description: The world-famous South Indian vegetarian restaurant chain that started in Chennai. Known for its dosas, idlis, and south Indian meals.

name: Panna Meena Ka Kund
description: A stunning 16th-century stepwell with a geometric crisscross pattern of stairs. A hidden gem near Amber Fort that has become a favorite for photographers.

name: Galtaji Temple
description: An ancient Hindu pilgrimage site set amidst rocky hills with natural spring water kunds. Also known as the Monkey Temple.

name: Sisodia Rani Garden
description: Built in 1728 by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II for his queen, this multi-tiered garden features murals, fountains, and Mughal-style landscaping.

name: Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah
description: Often called the Baby Taj, this is an exquisite Mughal tomb made entirely of white marble with intricate pietra dura inlay work. Built before the Taj Mahal.

name: Kinari Bazaar Agra
description: One of the oldest and busiest markets in Agra, famous for marble handicrafts, petha sweets, leather goods, and embroidered fabrics.

name: Partition Museum
description: The world's first Partition Museum documenting the stories and artifacts from the 1947 partition of India. A deeply moving experience.

name: Maharaja Ranjit Singh Museum
description: Housed in a summer palace, this museum displays weapons, paintings, and artifacts from the Sikh Empire era under Maharaja Ranjit Singh.

name: Gobindgarh Fort
description: An 18th-century Sikh fortress now converted into a heritage experience with 7D theatre, war galleries, and live performances showcasing Punjab's history.

name: Nandi Hills
description: A scenic hill fortress famous for its breathtaking sunrise views, paragliding opportunities, and pleasant weather. Located 60 km from Bangalore.

name: Santhome Cathedral Basilica
description: A 16th-century cathedral built over the tomb of St. Thomas, one of the twelve apostles of Jesus. One of only three churches in the world built over the tomb of an apostle.

name: Guindy National Park
description: One of the smallest national parks in India located inside the city. Home to blackbuck, spotted deer, jackals, and over 150 species of birds.

name: DakshinaChitra Heritage Museum
description: A living heritage museum showcasing the art, architecture, crafts, and performing arts of South India through reconstructed traditional houses.

name: Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park
description: High-altitude zoo housing snow leopards, red pandas, Tibetan wolves, and Himalayan black bears. The snow leopard breeding program here is globally renowned.

name: Glenary's Bakery & Cafe
description: A colonial-era bakery and restaurant established in 1935, serving freshly baked pastries and multi-cuisine food with panoramic mountain views from the terrace.

name: Karim's Old Delhi
description: A legendary Mughal cuisine restaurant established in 1913, serving recipes passed down from royal cooks of the Mughal court. Famous for its mutton burra.

name: Spice Plantation Tour
description: Visit a working organic spice plantation growing cardamom, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla. Includes guided tours and traditional Goan lunch.

name: Laad Bazaar
description: A 200-year-old market near Charminar famous for lac bangles, pearls, and semi-precious stones. One of the oldest and most vibrant bazaars in India.

name: Nehru Zoological Park
description: One of the largest zoos in India spread over 380 acres. Houses white tigers, Indian rhinos, and a butterfly park. Features a toy train and safari rides.

name: Mandore Gardens
description: The ancient capital of Marwar featuring royal cenotaphs, gardens, and the Hall of Heroes with life-sized carvings of folk gods.

name: Rao Jodha Desert Rock Park
description: A 72-hectare ecopark at the base of Mehrangarh Fort showcasing the ecology of a rocky desert. Home to over 200 species of native plants.

name: Fort Kochi Beach
description: A charming beach promenade with Chinese fishing nets, colonial architecture, and stunning sunsets. The area has a European feel with art galleries and cafes.

name: Kerala Folklore Museum
description: A unique private museum showcasing 4000 years of Kerala's folk heritage, including tribal costumes, temple carvings, and antique jewelry.

name: Mattancherry Spice Market
description: The historic center of India's spice trade. Walk through narrow lanes filled with the aroma of cardamom, cloves, pepper, and cinnamon.

name: Marble Palace
description: A pristine neoclassical mansion from 1835 housing original paintings by Rubens and Reynolds, Egyptian antiquities, and rare marble from 90 varieties.

name: Peter Cat Restaurant
description: An iconic Park Street restaurant since 1975, legendary for its Chelo Kebab. A must-visit for anyone exploring Kolkata's food scene.

name: Great Himalayan National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site spanning 754 sq km of pristine alpine forests. Home to snow leopards, western tragopan, and over 375 plant species.

name: Kullu Shawl Factory
description: A handloom factory where you can watch traditional Kullu shawls being woven on wooden looms and buy directly from the weavers.

name: Bijli Mahadev Temple
description: Perched at 2460m, this temple is struck by lightning regularly, shattering the Shiva lingam inside which is reassembled by the priest using butter.

name: Raghunath Temple Kullu
description: The principal deity of the Kullu Valley, this temple is the starting point of the famous Dussehra celebrations that last for seven days.

name: Thirumalai Nayakkar Palace
description: A 17th-century Indo-Saracenic marvel with massive pillars, stucco work, and a sound-and-light show narrating the history of the Nayak dynasty.

name: Gandhi Memorial Museum Madurai
description: A powerful museum housing the bloodstained dhoti worn by Mahatma Gandhi when he was assassinated, along with exhibits on India's freedom struggle.

name: Banana Leaf Restaurant Madurai
description: An authentic South Indian meals restaurant serving traditional Tamil cuisine on banana leaves. Famous for its thali, filterc coffee, and jigarthanda dessert.

name: Jogini Waterfall
description: A 160-foot waterfall accessible via a scenic trek through pine forests from Vashisht village. The trek itself is an adventure through beautiful terrain.

name: Dharavi Slum Tour
description: A respectful guided walking tour through Asia's largest slum, showcasing its thriving leather, pottery, and recycling industries generating $500M+ annually.

name: Top Station
description: The highest point on the Munnar-Kodaikanal road at 1880m, offering panoramic views of the Tamil Nadu plains and the Western Ghats valley.

name: Anamudi Peak Viewpoint
description: Viewpoint for South India's highest peak at 2695m located in Eravikulam National Park. Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr.

name: Tata Tea Museum
description: A museum tracing the history of tea cultivation in Munnar. Includes processing demonstrations, antique machinery, and tea tasting sessions.

name: Mysore Sand Sculpture Museum
description: India's first ever sand sculpture museum featuring intricate sand art depicting mythology, wildlife, and famous personalities.

name: Tiffin Top (Dorothy's Seat)
description: A scenic viewpoint accessible via a 4km trek from Ayarpatta, offering 360-degree views of the Himalayas and Nainital Lake.

name: Ooty Botanical Gardens
description: A stunning 55-acre garden established in 1848, featuring a 20-million-year-old fossilized tree trunk and over 650 species of plants, ferns, and orchids.

name: Pykara Lake and Falls
description: A scenic lake surrounded by shola forests offering boating and a nearby waterfall. A peaceful retreat away from the tourist crowds of Ooty town.

name: Toda Hut Museum
description: A museum showcasing the unique culture of the Toda tribe, the indigenous inhabitants of the Nilgiri Hills, with their distinctive barrel-shaped huts.

name: Savitri Temple Pushkar
description: Perched atop a hill, this temple offers the most spectacular sunset views over Pushkar Lake. The ropeway/cable car ride adds to the experience.

name: Rose Garden Pushkar
description: A beautiful garden with hundreds of varieties of roses. The rose essence and rose water produced here are exported globally.

name: Pushkar Bazaar
description: A vibrant bazaar along the main street selling silver jewelry, leather bags, ethnic clothing, and handicrafts. Best experienced during sunset hours.

name: Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia)
description: The abandoned Maharishi Mahesh Yogi ashram where The Beatles stayed in 1968. Covered in striking graffiti art, it's now an open-air gallery in the forest.

name: Neer Garh Waterfall
description: A two-tiered waterfall accessible via a short trek from the main road. A refreshing spot for swimming in natural rock pools.

name: Kalka-Shimla Railway
description: A UNESCO World Heritage narrow-gauge railway covering 96 km through 102 tunnels and over 800 bridges. One of India's most scenic train journeys.

name: Jakhu Temple
description: Perched at 2455m, the highest point in Shimla, featuring a 108-foot Hanuman statue — the tallest in the world. Offers panoramic views of surrounding peaks.

name: Indian Institute of Advanced Study
description: A stunning neo-Gothic building originally the Viceregal Lodge — the former summer residence of British viceroys. Used as the set for many Bollywood films.

name: Sajjangarh Palace (Monsoon Palace)
description: A hilltop palace built to watch approaching monsoon clouds. Offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Aravalli range and Udaipur's lakes at sunset.

name: Vintage Car Museum Udaipur
description: Houses a collection of vintage Rolls-Royces, Cadillacs, and Mercedes owned by the Mewar royal family, including a solar-powered car.

name: Ambrai Ghat
description: A scenic waterfront area on Lake Pichola offering the most photographed view of Lake Palace and City Palace illuminated at night.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring 30 rock-cut Buddhist cave monuments from the 2nd century BC. The murals here are considered masterpieces of Buddhist art.

name: Ellora Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with 34 caves representing Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain art from the 6th-11th century. The Kailasa Temple is carved from a single rock.

name: Bibi Ka Maqbara
description: Known as the Taj of the Deccan, this 17th-century mausoleum was built by Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb's son in memory of his mother. A striking replica of the Taj Mahal.

name: Daulatabad Fort
description: A 14th-century hilltop fortress considered one of the most impregnable forts in India. Features a dark spiral tunnel passage, moats, and impressive bastions.

name: Panchakki Water Mill
description: A 17th-century water mill that used an underground water channel from a mountain spring to grind grain. A remarkable feat of medieval engineering.

name: Tandoor Restaurant Aurangabad
description: A popular restaurant known for Mughlai and Hyderabadi cuisine. Famous for naan-e-khalas, biryani, and the Deccan-style kebabs.

name: Khajuraho Temples
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring 25 temples with stunning erotic sculptures from the Chandela dynasty (950-1050 AD). The finest examples of Indian medieval art.

name: Panna National Park
description: A tiger reserve and national park near Khajuraho featuring the Ken River gorge, waterfalls, and a thriving tiger population successfully reintroduced.

name: Raneh Falls
description: A 5-km long canyon of crystalline granite formations in stunning shades of pink, red, and grey, with the Ken River cascading as waterfalls between them.

name: Light and Sound Show Khajuraho
description: An immersive evening show narrating the history of the Chandela kings and the making of the Khajuraho temples through lights, sounds, and projections.

name: Raja Cafe Khajuraho
description: A rooftop cafe directly facing the Western Group of temples. Known for its banana pancakes, Israeli food, and stunning temple views during sunset.

name: Sanchi Stupa
description: UNESCO World Heritage Buddhist monument dating to the 3rd century BC, commissioned by Emperor Ashoka. The Great Stupa is the oldest stone structure in India.

name: Bhimbetka Rock Shelters
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with 30,000-year-old cave paintings — the oldest known rock art in India. Over 700 rock shelters showcasing Stone Age art.

name: Upper Lake Bhopal
description: Asia's largest artificial lake created in the 11th century. Offers boating, jet skiing, and a scenic promenade with views of the old city skyline.

name: Tribal Museum Bhopal
description: A stunning modern museum showcasing the art, culture, and lifestyle of Madhya Pradesh's tribal communities through immersive installations.

name: Taj-ul-Masajid
description: The largest mosque in India and one of the largest in Asia. Its pink facade with two white-domed minarets is an iconic Bhopal landmark.

name: Under the Mango Tree Restaurant
description: A charming restaurant known for Bhopali cuisine including biryani, rogan josh, and korma. Set in a heritage haveli ambiance.

name: Ranthambore National Park
description: One of India's premier tiger reserves spread over 392 sq km. Known for relaxed tiger sightings with the majestic Ranthambore Fort in the backdrop.

name: Ranthambore Fort
description: A UNESCO World Heritage hilltop fort dating to the 10th century inside the national park. Offers incredible views and ancient Jain temples.

name: Surwal Lake
description: A scenic lake near Ranthambore that attracts migratory birds in winter. A great spot for bird watching and photography.

name: The Tigress Restaurant
description: A wildlife-themed restaurant near the park entrance serving authentic Rajasthani laal maas, dal baati churma, and kebabs in a rustic jungle setting.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
description: The Venice of the East — a network of lagoons, lakes, and canals best experienced on a houseboat. A UNESCO-nominated world heritage water system.

name: Alleppey Beach
description: A tranquil beach famous for its century-old pier extending into the sea. The lighthouse here offers panoramic views of the coastline.

name: Krishnapuram Palace
description: An 18th-century Kerala-style palace housing a museum with bronze sculptures, antique furniture, and a renowned 49-sq-ft mural painting.

name: Revi Karunakaran Museum
description: A quirky private museum with a vast collection of rare Swarovski crystals, ivory carvings, Chinese porcelain, and antique clocks.

name: Toddy Shop Alleppey
description: An authentic Kerala toddy shop serving fresh toddy (palm wine) with spicy tapioca and Kerala fish curry. A genuine local cultural experience.

name: Jim Corbett National Park
description: India's oldest national park established in 1936, named after hunter-turned-conservationist Jim Corbett. Home to Royal Bengal Tigers and 600+ bird species.

name: Garjiya Devi Temple
description: A temple perched on a large rock in the middle of the Kosi River near the park entrance. Offers scenic views of the river and surrounding forests.

name: Corbett Museum
description: Located in Jim Corbett's winter home, this museum displays his personal belongings, letters, photographs, and hunting memorabilia.

name: Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep)
description: Home to Radhanagar Beach, voted Asia's best beach. Crystal clear turquoise waters, white sand, and world-class scuba diving and snorkeling.

name: Cellular Jail
description: A colonial-era political prison known as Kala Pani. The light and sound show narrating freedom fighters' struggles is deeply moving.

name: Ross Island
description: Once the administrative headquarters of the British in Andaman, now in atmospheric ruins reclaimed by roots of banyan trees and wild deer.

name: North Bay Island
description: A coral island known for glass-bottom boat rides, sea walking, and snorkeling among colorful coral reefs and tropical fish.

name: Baratang Island Limestone Caves
description: Stunning limestone caves formed over thousands of years, accessible via a thrilling speedboat ride through dense mangrove creeks.

name: New Lighthouse Restaurant
description: A popular seafood restaurant in Port Blair known for grilled lobster, crab masala, and fresh fish thali served with local flavors.

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site — the place where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree. The most sacred Buddhist pilgrimage site in the world.

name: Bodhi Tree
description: A direct descendant of the original sacred fig tree under which the Buddha sat for seven days and attained Nirvana 2500+ years ago.

name: Great Buddha Statue
description: A 25-metre-high statue of Buddha in the meditation pose, one of the tallest Buddha statues in India. Surrounded by a beautiful garden.

name: Royal Bhutan Monastery
description: One of many international monasteries in Bodh Gaya, representing Bhutanese Buddhist architecture with intricate paintings and prayer wheels.

name: Kaziranga National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site home to two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses. Also hosts tigers, elephants, and wild water buffalo.

name: Kaziranga Elephant Safari
description: An early morning elephant-back safari through the tall grasslands for up-close encounters with rhinos. A unique and unforgettable wildlife experience.

name: Orchid Park Kaziranga
description: A beautiful park showcasing over 500 varieties of orchids along with other Assamese flora. Also has a small museum on the park's biodiversity.

name: Wild Grass Lodge Restaurant
description: A renowned eco-resort restaurant serving authentic Assamese cuisine — duck curry, fish tenga, and bamboo shoot dishes in a jungle-themed setting.

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage 13th-century temple designed as a massive chariot of the Sun God with 12 pairs of exquisitely carved wheels and 7 horses. A masterpiece of Kalinga architecture.

name: Jagannath Temple Puri
description: One of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites and home to the annual Rath Yatra. The largest kitchen in the world feeds 10,000+ devotees daily.

name: Puri Beach
description: A long sandy beach famous for its sand art by Sudarsan Pattnaik, colorful fisherman boats, and vibrant sunset views.

name: Chilika Lake
description: Asia's largest brackish water lagoon and a Ramsar wetland site. Home to Irrawaddy dolphins and over a million migratory birds in winter.

name: Wildgrass Restaurant Puri
description: A seaside restaurant known for Odia cuisine including dalma, pakhala bhata, chenna poda, and fresh seafood.

name: Statue of Unity
description: The world's tallest statue at 182 metres, depicting Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. Features a viewing gallery at 153m offering stunning views of the Narmada Valley.

name: Sabarmati Ashram
description: Mahatma Gandhi's home for 13 years from which he led the famous Dandi Salt March. A tranquil riverside ashram now housing a museum of Gandhi's life.

name: Adalaj Stepwell
description: A stunning 15th-century five-storey stepwell with intricately carved pillars, galleries, and platforms depicting mythological scenes. A masterpiece of Indo-Islamic architecture.

name: Calico Museum of Textiles
description: One of the world's finest textile museums showcasing Indian textiles from the 15th century including Mughal court textiles, religious hangings, and regional weaves.

name: Rani Ki Vav
description: UNESCO World Heritage 11th-century stepwell built as a memorial to a king. Features over 500 sculptures in 7 gallery levels — one of the finest in India.

name: Manek Chowk
description: A night market that transforms from a vegetable market to a jewelry market to a street food paradise. Famous for khaman, fafda, and pav bhaji.

name: Valley of Flowers National Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage alpine meadow at 3658m bursting with over 600 species of wildflowers during the monsoon season. A trekker's paradise.

name: Hemkund Sahib
description: A Sikh gurudwara at 4362m beside a glacial lake surrounded by seven snow-covered peaks. One of the highest places of worship in the world.

name: Chopta - Tungnath Trek
description: The Mini Switzerland of India at 2680m with a scenic trek to Tungnath — the highest Shiva temple in the world at 3680m and Chandrashila peak at 4000m.

name: Auli Ski Resort
description: India's premier skiing destination with slopes ranging from 2519m to 3049m. Features a 4km gondola cable car and stunning views of Nanda Devi peak.

name: Tehri Dam Lake
description: The lake behind one of the world's tallest dams offers jet skiing, speed boating, kayaking, and camping on the emerald green reservoir shores.

name: Bara Imambara
description: Grand Mughal shrine famous for its massive arched hall and labyrinthine Bhool Bhulaiya maze on the upper floors.

name: Chota Imambara
description: Ornate golden-domed palace of lights adorned with Belgian chandeliers, gilt mirrors, and intricate calligraphy.

name: Rumi Darwaza
description: Imposing 60-foot Awadhi gateway modelled after a Turkish gate in Constantinople, an iconic symbol of Lucknow.

name: British Residency
description: Haunting colonial ruins from the 1857 siege with a museum chronicling the Indian independence struggle.

name: Hazratganj Market
description: Lucknow's premier shopping boulevard lined with chikankari emporiums, bookstores, and legendary kebab shops.

name: Ambedkar Memorial Park
description: Vast red sandstone memorial park with elephant statues, reflecting pools, and impressive modern architecture.

name: Tunday Kababi
description: Legendary 100-year-old kebab shop famous for its melt-in-mouth galouti kebabs and Lucknowi biryani.

name: Janeshwar Mishra Park
description: Asia's largest public park spread over 376 acres with jogging tracks, boating lake, and amphitheater.

name: Rock Garden
description: Unique garden of sculptures and art installations made entirely from industrial and urban waste by Nek Chand.

name: Sukhna Lake
description: Picturesque man-made reservoir at the Shivalik foothills with boating, walking promenade, and bird sanctuary.

name: Capitol Complex
description: Le Corbusier's UNESCO World Heritage masterpiece comprising the High Court, Secretariat, and iconic Open Hand monument.

name: Rose Garden
description: Asia's largest rose garden spread over 30 acres with 1,600+ rose varieties and annual Rose Festival.

name: Elante Mall
description: North India's largest mall with international brands, multiplex, food court, and rooftop amusement zone.

name: Government Museum
description: Premier museum housing Gandhara sculptures, Pahari miniature paintings, and contemporary Indian art.

name: Jaisalmer Fort
description: UNESCO-listed 'Golden Fort' rising from the Thar Desert, one of the world's largest fully preserved fortified cities.

name: Patwon Ki Haveli
description: Cluster of five ornately carved sandstone mansions with intricate jharokhas, the largest havelis in Jaisalmer.

name: Sam Sand Dunes
description: Iconic Thar Desert dunes offering camel safaris, desert camping under stars, and traditional Rajasthani cultural evenings.

name: Gadisar Lake
description: Serene medieval rainwater harvesting lake surrounded by temples, shrines, and ghats with pedalboat rides.

name: Bada Bagh
description: Royal cenotaph garden with intricately carved sandstone chhatris of Jaisalmer's Maharajas against desert sunset.

name: Kuldhara Abandoned Village
description: Mysterious 13th-century ghost village abandoned overnight by its Brahmin inhabitants, now an eerie heritage site.

name: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi
description: Sacred birthplace of Lord Krishna with ancient prison cell, ornate temple, and engaging Krishna Leela museum.

name: Dwarkadhish Temple
description: Magnificent 1814 Rajasthani-style temple with carved pillars, painted ceilings, and spectacular Holi celebrations.

name: Vishram Ghat
description: Most sacred ghat on the Yamuna where Krishna rested after slaying Kansa, with evening Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Government Museum Mathura
description: Outstanding collection of Mathura School of Art sculptures including rare Kushan-era Buddhas and Hindu deities.

name: Banke Bihari Temple
description: Vrindavan's most popular temple with an enchanting black idol of Krishna and high-energy devotional atmosphere.

name: ISKCON Vrindavan
description: Modern Hare Krishna temple complex with marble architecture, multimedia shows, and international vegetarian restaurant.

name: Prem Mandir
description: Spectacular white marble temple illuminated spectacularly at night, depicting scenes from Krishna's life.

name: Robber's Cave
description: Natural limestone cave formation with a stream flowing through it, perfect for wading and picnicking.

name: Sahastradhara
description: Cascading sulphur springs and waterfalls believed to have healing properties, with ropeway rides and caves.

name: Forest Research Institute
description: Stunning Greco-Roman colonial building spread over 2,000 acres housing six museums on India's forestry heritage.

name: Mindrolling Monastery
description: One of the largest Buddhist centres outside Tibet with a 220-ft stupa, Japanese garden, and sacred murals.

name: Tapkeshwar Mahadev Temple
description: Ancient cave temple dedicated to Lord Shiva where natural spring water continuously drips on the Shivalinga.

name: Rajaji National Park
description: 820 sq km wildlife sanctuary at the Shivalik foothills with elephants, tigers, leopards, and 400+ bird species.

name: Shaniwar Wada
description: Grand 18th-century Peshwa palace-fortress with imposing gates and evening sound & light show.

name: Aga Khan Palace
description: Elegant Italian-arched palace where Mahatma Gandhi, Kasturba, and Mahadev Desai were imprisoned, now a Gandhi memorial.

name: Sinhagad Fort
description: Historic hilltop fort 1,312m above sea level offering panoramic views, tied to the legend of Tanaji Malusare.

name: Dagdusheth Halwai Ganpati
description: Pune's most revered and richly decorated Ganesh temple, epicenter of the grand Ganesh Chaturthi celebrations.

name: Osho Ashram
description: World-famous international meditation resort in a lush green campus with unique spiritual programs.

name: Lal Mahal
description: Recreation of the palace where young Shivaji spent his formative years, with exhibits of Maratha history.

name: Pataleshwar Cave Temple
description: 8th-century rock-cut Shiva temple carved from a single basalt rock, a peaceful oasis amid the bustling city.

name: Vetal Tekdi
description: Popular hilltop biodiversity park for morning jogging and sunset views over the city, with rich birdlife.

name: Rann of Kutch
description: Vast white salt marsh stretching to the horizon, especially magical during the Rann Utsav festival under full moon.

name: Kala Dungar
description: Highest point of Kutch at 462m offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Great Rann and Pakistan border.

name: Mandvi Beach
description: Pristine windswept beach known for traditional wooden shipbuilding yards and the stunning Vijay Vilas Palace.

name: Aina Mahal
description: 18th-century Hall of Mirrors palace showcasing fine Dutch tilework, Venetian glass, and Kutchi craftsmanship.

name: Bhuj Haat
description: Government handicraft centre showcasing exquisite Kutchi embroidery, bandhani, and rogan art from local artisans.

name: Laxmi Vilas Palace
description: Sprawling Indo-Saracenic palace four times the size of Buckingham Palace, still residence of the Gaekwad royal family.

name: Sayaji Baug
description: Grand public garden with zoo, planetarium, Baroda Museum with Egyptian mummies, and floral clock.

name: Champaner-Pavagadh
description: UNESCO World Heritage archaeological park with exquisite 16th-century Islamic architecture and hilltop Hindu temple.

name: Makarpura Palace
description: Elegant Italian-style summer palace of the Gaekwads set in beautiful gardens, now an Indian Air Force training academy.

name: Stone Chariot
description: Exquisite stone chariot-shrine dedicated to Garuda in the Vittala Temple complex, an icon of Hampi.

name: Hampi Bazaar
description: Grand colonnaded street that was once the thriving market of the Vijayanagara Empire, now lined with pillar ruins.

name: Elephant Stables
description: Row of 11 domed chambers that once housed the royal elephants, blending Hindu and Islamic architectural styles.

name: Tungabhadra Dam
description: Massive masonry dam with beautiful gardens, musical fountain, and boat rides on the reservoir.

name: Abbey Falls
description: Picturesque waterfall cascading through coffee and spice plantations into a rocky pool amidst lush greenery.

name: Raja's Seat
description: Scenic garden viewpoint where Kodagu kings once watched sunsets over mist-covered valleys and paddy fields.

name: Dubare Elephant Camp
description: River island elephant camp where visitors can bathe, feed, and interact with trained elephants on the Kaveri.

name: Talacauvery
description: Sacred origin of the River Kaveri at Brahmagiri Hill, a pilgrimage site with stunning hilltop views.

name: Namdroling Monastery
description: Largest Nyingma Buddhist monastery outside Tibet with stunning golden Buddha statues and vibrant Tibetan murals.

name: Promenade Beach
description: 1.5 km scenic rock beach promenade with colonial landmarks, Gandhi statue, and stunning sunrise views.

name: Auroville
description: Experimental international township centered around the golden Matrimandir meditation sphere, an UNESCO-supported project.

name: French Quarter
description: Charming colonial district with mustard-yellow French villas, bougainvillea lanes, and boutique cafes.

name: Sri Aurobindo Ashram
description: Serene ashram founded by philosopher Sri Aurobindo and The Mother, offering guided meditation and spiritual programs.

name: Paradise Beach
description: Secluded golden-sand beach accessible only by boat through backwaters, perfect for swimming and sunbathing.

name: Cafe des Arts
description: Iconic French Quarter cafe in a heritage villa serving crepes, continental breakfasts, and filter coffee.

name: Shore Temple
description: UNESCO 8th-century granite temple overlooking the Bay of Bengal, one of the oldest structural stone temples of South India.

name: Pancha Rathas
description: Five stunning monolithic rock-cut rathas (chariots) carved from single granite boulders in Dravidian style.

name: Arjuna's Penance
description: World's largest open-air rock relief carving — a 27m x 9m Pallava masterpiece depicting mythological scenes.

name: Krishna's Butter Ball
description: Giant 250-ton natural balancing boulder on a smooth slope that defies gravity, a geological wonder.

name: Tiger Cave
description: Pallava-era rock-cut shrine with elaborate tiger-head carvings framing the entrance, set near the beach.

name: Brihadeeswarar Temple
description: UNESCO masterpiece built by Raja Raja Chola I in 1010 AD with an 80-ton granite capstone atop a 216-ft vimana.

name: Thanjavur Royal Palace
description: Nayak-era palace complex with the Saraswathi Mahal Library housing rare palm-leaf manuscripts and Maratha art gallery.

name: Gangaikonda Cholapuram
description: Magnificent Chola temple rivalling Thanjavur's Big Temple, with exquisite Nataraja and Saraswati sculptures.

name: Edakkal Caves
description: Ancient caves at 1,200m with Stone Age petroglyphs dating back 6,000 years, reached by trekking through tea estates.

name: Banasura Sagar Dam
description: India's largest earth dam surrounded by misty mountains, with speed boating on the reservoir.

name: Meenmutty Falls
description: Spectacular 300m three-tiered waterfall reached through a moderate jungle trek, the largest falls in Wayanad.

name: Wayanad Wildlife Sanctuary
description: 344 sq km sanctuary contiguous with Bandipur and Nagarhole parks, home to elephants, tigers, and exotic birdlife.

name: Chembra Peak
description: Wayanad's highest peak at 2,100m famous for the heart-shaped lake en route, a popular trekking destination.

name: Lingaraj Temple
description: Bhubaneswar's grandest 11th-century temple with a towering 55m deul representing the pinnacle of Kalinga architecture.

name: Udayagiri & Khandagiri Caves
description: 2nd-century BCE Jain rock-cut caves with elaborate sculptures, inscriptions, and panoramic city views.

name: Dhauli Peace Pagoda
description: White peace pagoda at the site where Emperor Ashoka embraced Buddhism after the bloody Kalinga War.

name: Nandankanan Zoo
description: 400-hectare botanical garden and zoo famous for white tigers, including Asia's first successful captive breeding.

name: Rajarani Temple
description: 11th-century Love Temple famous for exquisitely carved sensuous nymph figures on its wine-red sandstone walls.

name: Sun Temple
description: UNESCO World Heritage 13th-century temple shaped as a colossal chariot of the Sun God with 24 carved wheels.

name: Konark Beach
description: Pristine golden beach near the Sun Temple where the annual Konark Dance Festival and international sand art festival are held.

name: ASI Archaeological Museum
description: Museum near the Sun Temple displaying salvaged temple sculptures, decorative panels, and archaeological finds.

name: Tsomgo Lake
description: Glacial lake at 12,313 feet surrounded by snow-capped mountains, frozen in winter and blooming with primula in spring.

name: Nathula Pass
description: Historic Indo-China border pass at 14,140 feet on the ancient Silk Route, open to Indian nationals with permits.

name: Rumtek Monastery
description: Largest monastery in Sikkim and seat of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism with stunning thangka paintings.

name: MG Marg
description: Gangtok's vibrant pedestrian-only shopping street with cafes, handicraft shops, and mountain views.

name: Hanuman Tok
description: Hilltop Hanuman temple maintained by the Indian Army offering panoramic views of Kanchenjunga range.

name: Do Drul Chorten
description: Sacred stupa with 108 prayer wheels, enshrining a rare set of dorjes and Guru Rinpoche's holy relics.

name: Umiam Lake
description: Sprawling reservoir surrounded by pine-clad hills, offering kayaking, boating, and scenic picnic spots.

name: Living Root Bridges
description: Unique bio-engineering marvel — bridges grown over centuries by training Ficus elastica roots across rivers by Khasi tribes.

name: Elephant Falls
description: Three-tiered waterfall cascading through lush forest, the most popular falls near Shillong city.

name: Don Bosco Museum
description: Seven-story museum showcasing the rich indigenous cultures, crafts, and traditions of all Northeast Indian tribes.

name: Dawki River
description: Crystal-clear river at the India-Bangladesh border famous for boats appearing to float in mid-air.

name: Rajwada Palace
description: 200-year-old seven-story Holkar-era palace blending Maratha, Mughal, and French architectural styles.

name: Sarafa Bazaar
description: India's most famous night street food market — a jewellery bazaar by day transforming into 150+ food stalls by night.

name: Lal Bagh Palace
description: Opulent Holkar dynasty palace with Italian marble floors, Bohemian chandeliers, and a replica Buckingham gate.

name: Patalpani Waterfall
description: Stunning 300-ft waterfall plunging into a deep gorge surrounded by rocky terrain, especially grand in monsoon.

name: Central Museum Indore
description: Museum housing rare Parmar-era sculptures, Hindu and Jain artefacts, and prehistoric tools from the Narmada valley.

name: Kandariya Mahadeva Temple
description: Largest and most ornate Chandela temple with 872 exquisitely carved statues including famous erotic sculptures.

name: Sanchi Stupa
description: UNESCO World Heritage Great Stupa built by Emperor Ashoka in 3rd century BCE, the oldest stone structure in India.

name: Sanchi Museum
description: ASI museum near the stupas with excavated Buddhist sculptures, relics, and replica of the famous Ashoka pillar.

name: Tawang Monastery
description: Largest monastery in India and 2nd largest in the world after Lhasa, perched at 10,000 ft with 400-year history.

name: Sela Pass
description: Breathtaking high-altitude pass at 13,700 ft with 101 sacred lakes, snow-covered landscapes, and war memorial.

name: Madhuri Lake
description: Stunning alpine lake formed by the 1950 earthquake, surrounded by snow peaks and named after Bollywood star Madhuri Dixit.

name: Tawang War Memorial
description: Moving memorial honoring 2,420 soldiers who fell during the 1962 Sino-Indian War, with sound and light show.

name: Kamakhya Temple
description: One of the oldest and most revered Shakti Peethas atop Nilachal Hill, centre of Tantric worship.

name: Umananda Island
description: Smallest inhabited river island on the Brahmaputra with a Shiva temple and golden langur sightings.

name: Assam State Museum
description: Museum showcasing Assamese sculptures, textiles, tribal artifacts, and natural history specimens.

name: Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Highest density of Indian one-horned rhinoceros in the world, a mini Kaziranga just 48 km from Guwahati.

name: DakshinaChitra
description: Living-history open-air museum showcasing traditional homes, crafts, and performing arts of South India.

name: Toorji Ka Jhalra
description: Beautifully restored 18th-century stepwell in the old city with intricately carved sandstone walls and cafes nearby.

name: Bagore Ki Haveli
description: Grand 18th-century haveli with 138 rooms housing costume exhibits plus a mesmerizing evening Dharohar dance show.

name: Vintage Car Museum
description: Royal collection of classic cars including a 1934 Rolls-Royce, 1938 Cadillac, and a solar-powered rickshaw.

name: Alappuzha Beach
description: Palm-fringed beach with an old lighthouse, pier stretching into the Arabian Sea, and colourful fishing boats.

name: Ramanathaswamy Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas with the world's longest temple corridor (1,212m) of ornately carved granite pillars.

name: Pamban Bridge
description: India's first sea bridge — a spectacular 2-km railway bridge over the Palk Strait connecting Rameswaram island.

name: Dhanushkodi
description: Haunting ghost town at India's tip destroyed by the 1964 cyclone, where the Bay of Bengal meets the Indian Ocean.

name: APJ Abdul Kalam Memorial
description: Glass-domed memorial museum dedicated to India's beloved Missile Man President with interactive exhibits.

name: Kodaikanal Lake
description: Star-shaped artificial lake surrounded by dense Shola forests, with pedal boating, cycling, and horse riding.

name: Coaker's Walk
description: Paved 1-km footpath along steep mountain slopes offering panoramic views of the plains 7,000 feet below.

name: Pillar Rocks
description: Three massive granite pillars rising 120m from a deep forested gorge, often shrouded in magical mist.

name: Bryant Park
description: Landscaped botanical garden on the lake shore with 325 species of hybrid roses and annual flower shows.

name: Bear Shola Falls
description: Secluded seasonal waterfall named after bears once spotted drinking here, surrounded by dense canopy.

name: Chamundi Hill Temple
description: Hilltop temple of goddess Chamundeshwari with a giant Nandi statue halfway up the 1,000 steps.

name: Mysore Zoo
description: One of India's oldest and best-maintained zoos (est. 1892) with elephants, giraffes, and white tigers.

name: The Ridge
description: Shimla's iconic open promenade with panoramic mountain views, colonial architecture, and the Christ Church.

name: Dalai Lama Temple Complex
description: Official residence of the 14th Dalai Lama and spiritual centre of Tibetan Buddhism in exile.

name: Bhagsu Waterfall
description: Popular waterfall near Bhagsunath Temple, a favourite among backpackers with a cafe perched at the top.

name: Triund Trek
description: Moderate 9-km trek to a stunning alpine meadow at 2,850m with 360-degree Dhauladhar range and valley views.

name: Norbulingka Institute
description: Centre preserving Tibetan culture with traditional arts, wood-carving workshops, and a Japanese-style garden.

name: HPCA Cricket Stadium
description: World's most picturesque cricket ground at 1,457m elevation with snow-clad Dhauladhar peaks as backdrop.

name: Neelkanth Mahadev Temple
description: Hilltop Shiva temple deep in the forest where Shiva drank the poison that turned his throat blue.

name: Tiffin Top
description: Hilltop viewpoint reached via pony ride through oak forests, offering panoramic views of Nainital and beyond.

name: Sidi Saiyyed Mosque
description: Famous for its exquisite stone lattice window (jali) depicting an intricate tree-of-life, icon of Ahmedabad.

name: Somnath Temple
description: One of the 12 sacred Jyotirlingas at the Arabian Sea shore, rebuilt multiple times, with spectacular light show.

name: Gir National Park
description: Only habitat of the Asiatic lion in the wild; 1,412 sq km sanctuary with jeep safari spotting 700+ lions.

name: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
description: World's richest and most visited Hindu temple atop the Seven Hills, attracting 50,000+ pilgrims daily.

name: Sri Venkateswara National Park
description: Biodiverse Eastern Ghats sanctuary with orchids, waterfalls, and trekking trails through dense deciduous forests.

name: Talakona Waterfall
description: Andhra Pradesh's highest waterfall at 270 feet, a trekking and nature lover's paradise inside the national park.

name: Chandragiri Fort
description: 11th-century fort where the British East India Company received the land grant for Madras (Chennai).

name: Araku Valley
description: Hill station at 1,300m in Eastern Ghats with coffee plantations, tribal museum, and scenic train journey.

name: Borra Caves
description: Million-year-old limestone caves at 1,400m with spectacular stalactite and stalagmite formations.

name: Kailasagiri
description: Hilltop park with giant Shiva-Parvati statues, ropeway ride over the Bay of Bengal, and panoramic views.

name: Submarine Museum
description: Decommissioned INS Kurusura submarine converted into a unique walk-through military museum on the beach.

name: Dal Lake
description: Iconic Kashmiri lake with ornate houseboats, floating vegetable markets, and mesmerizing Shikara rides.

name: Mughal Gardens (Nishat Bagh)
description: Garden of Joy — largest Mughal garden in Kashmir with 12 terraces symbolizing zodiac signs, overlooking Dal Lake.

name: Shalimar Bagh
description: Crown of Srinagar — Emperor Jahangir's garden of love with cascading fountains and chinars over Dal Lake.

name: Hazratbal Shrine
description: White marble mosque housing a relic believed to be a hair of Prophet Muhammad, set beautifully on Dal Lake.

name: Pari Mahal
description: Mughal-era terraced garden ruin known as the 'Palace of Fairies', perched above Srinagar with magical night illumination.

name: Pangong Tso Lake
description: Stunning high-altitude lake at 14,270 ft stretching from India to China, changing colours from blue to green.

name: Nubra Valley
description: Cold desert valley beyond Khardung La with sand dunes, double-humped Bactrian camels, and ancient monasteries.

name: Thiksey Monastery
description: 12-story monastery resembling Lhasa's Potala Palace with a 15m-tall Maitreya Buddha and stunning valley views.

name: Magnetic Hill
description: Gravity-defying hill where vehicles appear to roll uphill due to an optical illusion created by the surrounding terrain.

name: Shanti Stupa
description: Japanese-built white-domed peace stupa on a hilltop offering spectacular sunset views over Leh town.

name: Hemis Monastery
description: Largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh, famous for its annual masked dance festival and treasured thangkas.

name: Old Goa Churches
description: Cluster of UNESCO-listed 16th-17th century Portuguese churches including the grand Se Cathedral and St. Cajetan.

name: Spice Plantations
description: Guided tours through aromatic spice estates with lunch buffet, elephant bathing, and Ayurvedic spa options.

name: Chitrakote Falls
description: India's broadest waterfall known as the 'Niagara of India', a horseshoe-shaped cascade on the Indravati River.

name: Tirathgarh Falls
description: Multi-tiered milky waterfall cascading down 300 feet through dense sal forest inside Kanger Valley National Park.

name: Kanger Valley National Park
description: Biosphere reserve with limestone caves, underground streams, subterranean fish, and endemic Bastar hill myna.

name: Hundru Falls
description: Jharkhand's highest waterfall plunging 98 metres down rocky terrain on the Subarnarekha River.

name: Jonha Falls
description: Picturesque 43m waterfall with 722 steps leading to the base, surrounded by dense forests and a Buddhist shrine.

name: Patratu Valley
description: Scenic valley with serpentine mountain roads, reservoir, and lush green hills — Jharkhand's hidden gem.

name: Peace Pagoda Darjeeling
description: Japanese-built white peace pagoda with four golden Buddha avatars, offering serene views of the tea gardens.

name: Upper Lake
description: One of the largest artificial lakes in Asia, offering boat rides, water sports, and serene sunset views.

name: Qutb Shahi Tombs
description: Peaceful necropolis of the Golconda sultans with 30+ exquisite domed tombs and gardens near Golconda Fort.

name: Nalanda University Ruins
description: UNESCO ruins of the world's oldest university (5th century) that once hosted 10,000 scholars from across Asia.

name: Nalanda Museum
description: Museum housing excavated Buddhist bronzes, manuscripts, and terracotta artefacts from the ancient university.

name: Rajgir Hot Springs
description: Sacred thermal springs where Buddha once bathed, with stepped pools at varying temperatures.

name: Giant Buddha Statue
description: Magnificent 80-ft seated Buddha statue unveiled by the 14th Dalai Lama, visible across the plains.

name: Mawlynnong Village
description: Asia's cleanest village with bamboo dustbins, living root bridges, and crystal-clear natural swimming pools.

name: Mawsmai Cave
description: Well-lit limestone cave with spectacular formations, narrow passages, and dripping stalactites.

name: Ujjayanta Palace
description: Grand Mughal-style palace of the Tripura royals with tiled floors, throne room, and state museum inside.

name: Neermahal
description: India's largest water palace built on Rudrasagar Lake, blending Hindu and Islamic architecture.

name: Unakoti
description: Mysterious 7th-century rock-carved Shiva panels and massive reliefs in the jungle, India's best-kept archaeological secret.

name: Loktak Lake
description: World's only floating lake with circular floating islands (phumdis) and the Keibul Lamjao floating national park.

name: Kangla Fort
description: Ancient seat of the Meitei kings surrounded by a moat, with the iconic twin dragon (Kangla Sha) statues.

name: INA Memorial
description: Memorial at Moirang where the INA flag was first hoisted on Indian soil during WWII's Battle of Imphal.

name: Kohima War Cemetery
description: Beautifully maintained WWII cemetery honouring 1,420 Allied soldiers with the famous 'When You Go Home' epitaph.

name: Dzukou Valley
description: Valley of flowers of the Northeast at 2,452m, carpeted with exotic seasonal blooms and bamboo groves.

name: Phawngpui (Blue Mountain)
description: Mizoram's highest peak at 2,157m with rhododendron blooms, orchids, and panoramic views of Myanmar border.

name: Durtlang Hills
description: Scenic viewpoint on the northern outskirts of Aizawl with sweeping views of the city and surrounding hills.

name: Valley of Flowers
description: UNESCO World Heritage alpine meadow with 600+ flower species blooming July-September in the upper Himalayas.

name: Auli
description: India's premier ski resort at 2,500-3,050m with Asia's longest cable car and panoramic Nanda Devi views.

name: Chopta-Tungnath Trek
description: Trek to the world's highest Shiva temple at 3,680m through dense rhododendron forests with Himalayan views.

name: Pushkar Camel Fair Grounds
description: Desert ground hosting the world's largest camel fair each November with races, folk performances, and trading.

name: Dilwara Jain Temples
description: Five marble Jain temples renowned for the most intricate and detailed marble carvings in the world.

name: Nakki Lake
description: Sacred lake in Rajasthan's only hill station with boating and Toad Rock viewpoint above.

name: Guru Shikhar
description: Highest peak in the Aravalli Range at 1,722m with a Dattatreya temple and views stretching to the Thar Desert.

name: Radhanagar Beach
description: Asia's Best Beach — pristine white sand stretch with turquoise waters, lush forest backdrop, and glowing sunsets.

name: Scuba at Havelock
description: World-class scuba diving at coral reef walls with sea turtles, manta rays, and vibrant marine biodiversity.

name: Kaziranga Orchid Park
description: Park showcasing 500+ orchid species native to Northeast India alongside exotic fish and butterflies.

name: Raghurajpur Heritage Village
description: Artisan village where every household practices Pattachitra painting, palm-leaf etching, and stone carving.

name: Ranthambore Safari Zone 6
description: Premium safari zone famous for tiger sightings near the three lakes inside the national park.

name: Ajmer Sharif Dargah
description: One of India's most revered Sufi shrines housing the tomb of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti, visited by millions.

name: Ana Sagar Lake
description: 12th-century artificial lake with marble pavilions built by Jahangir and Daulat Bagh gardens.

name: Taragarh Fort
description: One of the oldest hill forts in India perched above the city with ancient water reservoirs and panoramic views.

name: Isha Yoga Center
description: Home of Adiyogi Shiva statue (112 ft) — world's largest bust (Guinness record), with meditation programs.

name: Marudamalai Temple
description: Ancient Murugan temple on a 500-ft hillock in the Western Ghats foothills with panoramic valley views.

name: Mahakaleshwar Temple
description: One of the 12 sacred Jyotirlingas with the famous Bhasma Aarti — pre-dawn worship with sacred ash.

name: Kal Bhairav Temple
description: Unique temple where the deity is offered liquor as prasad, an ancient Tantric Shiva tradition.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat Aarti
description: Mesmerizing nightly Ganga Aarti with synchronised fire rituals by trained priests on the steps of the Ganges.

name: Matrimandir
description: Golden dome meditation chamber at Auroville's heart with a crystal sphere bathed in sunlight — a spiritual marvel.

name: St. Philomena's Cathedral
description: One of India's tallest churches in Neo-Gothic style modelled after Cologne Cathedral, with stained glass windows.

name: Jim Corbett Museum
description: Museum in the bungalow of legendary hunter-conservationist Jim Corbett with his rifles, cameras, and writings.

name: Corbett Jeep Safari
description: 4x4 jungle safari through Dhikala and Bijrani zones spotting Bengal tigers, elephants, and crocodiles.

name: Coffee Plantation Walk
description: Guided walk through Arabica coffee estates learning harvest-to-cup process with pepper and cardamom plants.

name: Naggar Castle
description: Medieval stone-and-wood castle of the Kullu rajas with an art gallery and views of snow-capped peaks.

name: Houseboat Cruise
description: Signature Kerala experience — overnight stay in a Kettuvallam houseboat cruising palm-fringed backwater canals.

name: Jama Masjid
description: India's largest mosque built by Shah Jahan in 1644 with courtyard holding 25,000 devotees and minaret views.

name: Gurudwara Bangla Sahib
description: Golden-domed Sikh gurudwara known for its sacred pool, massive community kitchen feeding 10,000+ daily, and serene atmosphere.

name: Mother House
description: Headquarters of Mother Teresa's Missionaries of Charity, housing her tomb and a small moving museum.

name: Kankaria Lake
description: 15th-century lake with lakefront promenade, zoo, kids' city, balloon rides, and weekend cultural events.

name: Nathmal Ji Ki Haveli
description: Unique 19th-century sandstone haveli with asymmetric design — built by two brothers simultaneously from opposite sides.

name: Elliot's Beach
description: Quieter alternative to Marina Beach popular for morning walks, surfing lessons, and evening food trucks.

name: Birla Mandir Hyderabad
description: Hilltop white marble temple with beautiful carvings offering panoramic city views, brilliantly lit at night.

name: Diu Fort
description: Massive Portuguese sea fortress with lighthouse, cannons, and Arabian Sea views on three sides.

name: Nagoa Beach
description: Horseshoe-shaped beach with calm waters ideal for swimming, surrounded by palm groves and water sports.

name: Diu Museum
description: Museum inside the former Church of St Thomas with Portuguese colonial artifacts, wooden saints, and silverware.

name: Hazara Rama Temple
description: Royal temple with 108 bas-relief panels of the Ramayana epic and polished black stone pillars.

name: Lotus Palace
description: Ornamental pavilion with lotus-bud domes blending Hindu and Islamic styles, used for royal entertainment.

name: Manikarnika Ghat
description: Holiest cremation ground in Hinduism where eternal fire has burned for thousands of years on the Ganges.

name: Tulsi Manas Temple
description: Modern marble temple with verses of Tulsi Ramcharitmanas engraved on walls, built at Tulsidas writing spot.

name: Banaras Hindu University
description: Asias largest residential university with Vishwanath Temple, Bharat Kala Bhavan museum, and beautiful campus.

name: Laxman Jhula
description: Iconic 450-ft iron suspension bridge over the Ganges, believed to mark where Laxmana crossed on a jute rope.

name: Nisargadhama Island
description: River island connected by hanging bridge with bamboo cottages, deer park, and elephant rides in bamboo groves.
